violations.text: Observed insect control device stored above food prep area in the kitchen. Insect control devices shall be installed so that the devices are not located over a FOOD preparation area. PIC removed insect control device at time of inspection. Establishment removed the insect control device during this inspection and will relocate the insect control device to a different location, away from food prep areas. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2026

Raw animal foods (such as chicken) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as cooked meat) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. PIC removed the raw chicken during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC cleaned and sanitized slicer at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2026

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was (25) parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 pp. Establishment shall sanitize in 3-bay sink until dishwasher is serviced and in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2026

Kimchi was observed held at 58 degrees Fahrenheit located in cold hold unit. Broth held at 55 degrees Fahrenheit located in cold hold unit. Per interview PIC unit was accidently turned off. TCS food were out of temp. for less than 4 hours. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC moved TCS foods to walk-in cooler to rapidly cool. Unit was turned back on during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2026

(Kimchi) was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2026

(Kimchi) packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2026

The door to the outside, located in the (front area), is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2026

The knife is stored on an unclean surface (in-between prep units) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. PIC moved knife during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2026

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/18/2024

At the time of inspection, the dish machine was observed not dispensing the proper amount of chemical sanitizer during the sanitizing cycle. PIC is to call for adjustment to be made for proper chemical sanitizer dispensing. PIC to use 3 Bay Sink with bleach/chlorine sanitizer until unit is adjusted. New Violation. Correct By: 12/18/2024

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an Air Gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. Plumbing Personnel on site at time of inspection. Picture to be sent to inspector with proof of installation of the Air Gap. New Violation. Correct By: 12/18/2024
